items.name: fetch
items.description: Built-in HTTP/HTTPS client for server-side requests. Returns a Response-like object with ok, status, json(), text(), arrayBuffer(). Supports timeouts, abort signals, upload/download progress tracking, automatic JSON body serialization, and custom TLS options for HTTPS URLs.

methodReturns.description: HTTP status code of the response (e.g. `200`, `404`).
Status message corresponding to the status code (e.g. `"OK"`, `"Not Found"`).

methods.description: true if the request was over HTTPS.

methodReturns.description: `true` when the response was received over HTTPS.
Value of the named header, or `null` if not present.

methods.description: Read body as text. Returns Promise<string>.

methodReturns.description: Resolves with the full response body as a UTF-8 string.

methods.description: Read body as parsed JSON. Returns Promise<any>.

methodReturns.description: Resolves with the body parsed as JSON. Throws `SyntaxError` on invalid JSON.

methods.description: Read body as ArrayBuffer. Returns Promise<ArrayBuffer>.

methodReturns.description: Resolves with the raw response bytes as an `ArrayBuffer`.

options.notes: Request body. Objects are auto-serialized to JSON.

tips: Object bodies are auto-serialized to JSON and Content-Type is set automatically.
Use rejectUnauthorized: false only for development — never in production.
Timeout and AbortSignal can be used together — whichever fires first cancels the request.
